address.name: postcode
address.notes: bothify(random_element(postcode_formats)).upper() — applies both digit and letter substitution then uppercases

address.name: country_code
address.notes: Conditionally returns random_element(alpha_2_country_codes) or random_element(alpha_3_country_codes) based on the representation parameter

address.name: current_country_code
address.notes: Extracts the country code from self.__lang__ by splitting on '_' and taking index [1]

address.name: current_country
address.notes: Looks up country name from date_time.Provider.countries by matching alpha_2_code to current_country_code(), with ambiguity and not-found error handling

automotive.name: vin
automotive.notes: Generates 8 random VIN chars, 8 rear chars (4 letters + 4 digits), computes weighted checksum mod 11 for position 9, concatenates front+checksum+rear into a 17-char VIN.

bank.name: aba
bank.notes: Picks a fed routing prefix (1-12), numerifies 6 random digits, then computes a weighted checksum digit to produce a 9-digit ABA routing number.

bank.name: bban
bank.notes: Regex-substitutes '?' chars in bban_format with random uppercase letters, then calls numerify to expand '#' digit markers.

bank.name: iban
bank.notes: Generates a BBAN, computes ISO 7064 MOD-97 check digits using ALPHA mapping, and prepends the country code to produce a full IBAN.

bank.name: swift8
bank.notes: Delegates to swift(length=8, use_dataset=...) which constructs a SWIFT code from bank/country/location codes.

bank.name: swift11
bank.notes: Delegates to swift(length=11, primary=..., use_dataset=...) which constructs an 11-char SWIFT code, optionally forcing 'XXX' branch suffix.

bank.name: swift
bank.notes: Constructs an 8- or 11-character SWIFT code from randomly generated or dataset-sampled bank code (4 alpha), country_code (2), location code (2 alphanumeric), and optional branch code (3 alphanumeric or 'XXX').

barcode.name: ean
barcode.notes: Generates length-1 random digits, optionally overwrites leading digits with a randomly chosen prefix, then appends a weighted-sum check digit (EAN-8 or EAN-13).

barcode.name: ean8
barcode.notes: Delegates to _ean(8): 7 random digits, optional prefix overwrite, weighted-sum check digit appended.

barcode.name: ean13
barcode.notes: Delegates to _ean(13): 12 random digits, optional prefix overwrite, weighted-sum check digit appended.

barcode.name: localized_ean
barcode.notes: Delegates to _ean with self.local_prefixes; random digits + random choice from local_prefixes + weighted-sum check digit.

barcode.name: localized_ean8
barcode.notes: Delegates to localized_ean(8): EAN-8 with local_prefixes and checksum.

barcode.name: localized_ean13
barcode.notes: Delegates to localized_ean(13): EAN-13 with local_prefixes and checksum.

color.name: hex_color
color.notes: Returns '#' + random_int(1, 16777215) formatted as a 6-digit lowercase hex string

color.name: safe_hex_color
color.notes: Generates 3 independent web-safe channel values via random_int(0,15)*17, formats each as 2-digit hex

color.name: rgb_color
color.notes: Joins 3 independent random_int(0,255) values as a comma-separated string

color.name: rgb_css_color
color.notes: Returns css rgb() function string with 3 independent random_int(0,255) channel values

color.name: color
color.notes: Delegates to RandomColor.generate() which uses HSV color model with hue/luminosity constraints and converts to the requested color_format

company.name: catch_phrase
company.notes: Joins one random element from each sub-list in catch_phrase_words tuple with a space

company.name: bs
company.notes: Joins one random element from each sub-list in bsWords tuple with a space

credit_card.name: credit_card_provider
credit_card.notes: Picks a random card type from credit_card_types then returns its .name attribute

credit_card.name: credit_card_number
credit_card.notes: Picks card type, picks random prefix, pads with random digits to card length, appends Luhn check digit

credit_card.name: credit_card_expire
credit_card.notes: Generates a random datetime between start and end via date_time_between, then formats with strftime(date_format)

credit_card.name: credit_card_full
credit_card.notes: Combines card name, parsed first+last name, credit_card_number, credit_card_expire, security code label and value into a multi-line string

credit_card.name: credit_card_security_code
credit_card.notes: Looks up security_code_length from the card type then calls numerify with that many # characters

currency.name: currency_code
currency.notes: Returns index [0] of self.currency(), which is a random element of currencies

currency.name: currency_name
currency.notes: Returns index [1] of self.currency(), which is a random element of currencies

currency.name: currency_symbol
currency.notes: Picks a random key from currency_symbols dict (or validates supplied code), returns dict lookup with fallback ¤

currency.name: cryptocurrency_code
currency.notes: Returns index [0] of self.cryptocurrency(), which is a random element of cryptocurrencies

currency.name: cryptocurrency_name
currency.notes: Returns index [1] of self.cryptocurrency(), which is a random element of cryptocurrencies

currency.name: pricetag
currency.notes: Picks random element from currencies for the code, concatenates with NO-BREAK SPACE and numerify(random_element(price_formats))

date_time.name: unix_time
date_time.notes: Returns a random float UNIX timestamp between _parse_start_datetime and _parse_end_datetime via _rand_seconds

date_time.name: time_delta
date_time.notes: Returns a timedelta object of random duration between now and end_datetime via _rand_seconds

date_time.name: date_time
date_time.notes: Returns datetime(1970,1,1) + timedelta(seconds=unix_time()) between epoch and end_datetime

date_time.name: date_time_ad
date_time.notes: Returns datetime between 0001-01-01 and end_datetime by adding random seconds to epoch

date_time.name: iso8601
date_time.notes: Returns date_time().isoformat(sep, timespec) as ISO 8601 string

date_time.name: date
date_time.notes: Returns date_time().strftime(pattern) string for a date between epoch and end_datetime

date_time.name: date_object
date_time.notes: Returns date_time().date() as a dtdate object between epoch and end_datetime

date_time.name: time
date_time.notes: Returns date_time().time().strftime(pattern) string for a random time

date_time.name: time_object
date_time.notes: Returns date_time().time() as a dttime object

date_time.name: date_time_between
date_time.notes: Returns a random datetime between start_date and end_date using _rand_seconds on parsed timestamps

date_time.name: date_between
date_time.notes: Returns a random dtdate between start_date and end_date via date_between_dates

date_time.name: future_datetime
date_time.notes: Returns date_time_between(start_date='+1s', end_date=end_date)

date_time.name: future_date
date_time.notes: Returns date_between(start_date='+1d', end_date=end_date)

date_time.name: past_datetime
date_time.notes: Returns date_time_between(start_date=start_date, end_date='-1s')

date_time.name: past_date
date_time.notes: Returns date_between(start_date=start_date, end_date='-1d')

date_time.name: date_time_between_dates
date_time.notes: Returns random datetime between datetime_start and datetime_end with optional tzinfo using _rand_seconds

date_time.name: date_between_dates
date_time.notes: Returns date_time_between_dates(date_start, date_end).date()

date_time.name: date_time_this_century
date_time.notes: Returns random datetime within the current century, controlled by before_now/after_now flags

date_time.name: date_time_this_decade
date_time.notes: Returns random datetime within the current decade, controlled by before_now/after_now flags

date_time.name: date_time_this_year
date_time.notes: Returns random datetime within the current year, controlled by before_now/after_now flags

date_time.name: date_time_this_month
date_time.notes: Returns random datetime within the current month, controlled by before_now/after_now flags

date_time.name: date_this_century
date_time.notes: Returns random dtdate within the current century, controlled by before_today/after_today flags

date_time.name: date_this_decade
date_time.notes: Returns random dtdate within the current decade, controlled by before_today/after_today flags

date_time.name: date_this_year
date_time.notes: Returns random dtdate within the current year, controlled by before_today/after_today flags

date_time.name: date_this_month
date_time.notes: Returns random dtdate within the current month, controlled by before_today/after_today flags

date_time.name: time_series
date_time.notes: Generator yielding (datetime, value) tuples at regular precision intervals between start_date and end_date

date_time.name: am_pm
date_time.notes: Returns self.date('%p') — AM or PM string derived from a random date_time()

date_time.name: day_of_month
date_time.notes: Returns self.date('%d') — two-digit day string derived from a random date_time()

date_time.name: day_of_week
date_time.notes: Returns self.date('%A') — full weekday name derived from a random date_time()

date_time.name: month
date_time.notes: Returns self.date('%m') — two-digit month string derived from a random date_time()

date_time.name: month_name
date_time.notes: Returns self.date('%B') — full month name derived from a random date_time()

date_time.name: year
date_time.notes: Returns self.date('%Y') — four-digit year string derived from a random date_time()

date_time.name: timezone
date_time.notes: Picks a random Country from self.countries then randomly selects one of its timezones strings

date_time.name: pytimezone
date_time.notes: Returns zoneinfo.ZoneInfo(self.timezone()) — a tzinfo object wrapping the random timezone string

date_time.name: date_of_birth
date_time.notes: Returns a dtdate for a random DOB constrained by minimum_age/maximum_age using change_year and date_time_ad

doi.name: doi
doi.notes: Builds DOI by concatenating fixed prefix '10', a random integer (1000-99999999) as registrant, and a bothify '?#?#?##' suffix lowercased.

file.name: mime_type
file.notes: picks a random category from mime_types keys (or uses provided category), then returns a random element from that category's tuple

file.name: file_name
file.notes: calls file_extension(category) for extension if not provided, then generator.word() for the base name, and joins them with a dot

file.name: file_extension
file.notes: picks a random category from file_extensions keys if none given, then returns random element from that category's extensions tuple

file.name: file_path
file.notes: builds a path of depth-many word() segments joined by OS separator, prepended with root if absolute, with optional traversal elements and extension handling

file.name: unix_device
file.notes: picks random prefix from unix_device_prefixes (or uses given prefix), picks random lowercase letter suffix, returns /dev/{prefix}{suffix}

file.name: unix_partition
file.notes: calls unix_device(prefix) then appends a random digit

geo.name: coordinate
geo.notes: Returns a Decimal with 6 decimal places: random int in [-180000000,180000000]/1000000 if no center, or uniform(center-radius, center+radius) if center given.

geo.name: latitude
geo.notes: Returns coordinate() divided by 2 to constrain range to [-90, 90].

geo.name: local_latlng
geo.notes: Filters land_coords by country_code, picks random_element, returns full tuple or coords-only tuple; returns None if no match.

geo.name: location_on_land
geo.notes: Picks random_element from land_coords and returns full PlaceType tuple or just (lat, lon) depending on coords_only flag.

internet.name: email
internet.notes: Conditionally builds email from user_name + safe_domain, free domain, or parses email_formats template depending on safe/domain params

internet.name: safe_email
internet.notes: Concatenates user_name() + '@' + safe_domain_name()

internet.name: free_email
internet.notes: Concatenates user_name() + '@' + free_email_domain()

internet.name: company_email
internet.notes: Concatenates user_name() + '@' + domain_name()

internet.name: ascii_email
internet.notes: Picks random email_formats template, parses it, then converts result to ASCII via _to_ascii

internet.name: ascii_safe_email
internet.notes: Concatenates user_name() + '@' + safe_domain_name() then converts to ASCII

internet.name: ascii_free_email
internet.notes: Concatenates user_name() + '@' + free_email_domain() then converts to ASCII

internet.name: ascii_company_email
internet.notes: Concatenates user_name() + '@' + domain_name() then converts to ASCII

internet.name: user_name
internet.notes: Picks random user_name_formats template, parses it, applies bothify, lowercases, then _to_ascii

internet.name: hostname
internet.notes: Picks random hostname_prefixes element + '-' + numerify('##'), optionally appends '.' + domain_name(levels)

internet.name: domain_name
internet.notes: Recursively builds domain: domain_word() + '.' + tld() or domain_word() + '.' + domain_name(levels-1)

internet.name: domain_word
internet.notes: Generates company name, splits on space, takes first element, converts to ASCII slug

internet.name: dga
internet.notes: Domain generation algorithm using bitwise arithmetic on year/month/day seed to produce a domain string of given length

internet.name: http_status_code
internet.notes: Returns random_int(100-599) if include_unassigned else random_element(http_assigned_codes)

internet.name: url
internet.notes: Builds URL by combining a random scheme with a random url_formats template, then parses it

internet.name: ipv4_network_class
internet.notes: Returns random_element('abc') — single character from the string 'abc'

internet.name: ipv4
internet.notes: Delegates to ipv4_private or ipv4_public or all-networks weighted random address/CIDR generation

internet.name: ipv4_private
internet.notes: Picks weighted random address from private IPv4 networks, optionally returns CIDR network notation

internet.name: ipv4_public
internet.notes: Picks weighted random address from public IPv4 networks (excluding private blocks), optionally returns CIDR

internet.name: ipv6
internet.notes: Generates random integer in IPv6 space (above IPv4 range), converts to IPv6Address string, optionally appends random prefix length as network

internet.name: mac_address
internet.notes: Generates 6 random bytes with first byte constrained to even (unicast) or odd (multicast) range, formats as colon-separated hex

internet.name: port_number
internet.notes: Returns random_int in system (0-1023), user (1024-49151), dynamic (49152-65535), or full (0-65535) range based on flags

internet.name: uri_path
internet.notes: Joins 1-3 (or deep) random elements from uri_paths with '/' separator

internet.name: uri
internet.notes: Builds URI by combining scheme + url_formats pattern parsed, then appends uri_path + uri_page + uri_extension

internet.name: slug
internet.notes: Generates a lorem text of ~20 chars using USLoremProvider word list, then slugifies it

internet.name: image_url
internet.notes: Picks random image_placeholder_services template (or uses provided one) and formats it with random or given width/height

internet.name: iana_id
internet.notes: Returns str(random_int(min=1, max=8888888))

internet.name: ripe_id
internet.notes: Builds 'ORG-{2-4 letters}{1-5 digits}-RIPE' via bothify with dynamically constructed format string

internet.name: nic_handle
internet.notes: Builds '{2-4 letters}{1-5 digits}-{suffix}' via bothify with dynamically constructed format string

internet.name: nic_handles
internet.notes: Returns a list of count nic_handle() results

isbn.name: isbn13
isbn.notes: Builds ISBN-13 by randomly selecting EAN prefix and registration group from rules dict, generating a numerified registrant/publication string, splitting via range rules, then computing and appending a check digit via ISBN13.format(separator).

isbn.name: isbn10
isbn.notes: Builds ISBN-10 by randomly selecting EAN prefix and registration group from rules dict, generating a numerified registrant/publication string, splitting via range rules, then computing and appending a check digit via ISBN10.format(separator).

job.name: job_female
job.notes: Returns random_element(jobs_female) if that locale attribute exists, otherwise delegates to job()

job.name: job_male
job.notes: Returns random_element(jobs_male) if that locale attribute exists, otherwise delegates to job()

lorem.name: word
lorem.notes: Calls get_words_list() then words(1, word_list)[0] — random_choices of length 1 from word_list (or ext_word_list / part_of_speech subset)

lorem.name: words
lorem.notes: Returns nb random words via random_choices (duplicates allowed) or random_sample (unique=True) from the resolved word list

lorem.name: sentence
lorem.notes: Picks nb_words words (optionally randomized ±40%), capitalises first word, joins with word_connector, appends sentence_punctuation

lorem.name: sentences
lorem.notes: Returns a list of nb sentence() results

lorem.name: paragraph
lorem.notes: Joins nb_sentences sentences (optionally randomized ±40%) with word_connector into a single string

lorem.name: paragraphs
lorem.notes: Returns a list of nb paragraph() results

lorem.name: text
lorem.notes: Branches on max_nb_chars (<25: words, <100: sentences, >=100: paragraphs), accumulates until size >= max_nb_chars then pops last element

lorem.name: texts
lorem.notes: Returns a list of nb_texts text() results each capped at max_nb_chars

misc.name: boolean
misc.notes: Returns random bool by comparing randint(1,100) against chance_of_getting_true parameter

misc.name: null_boolean
misc.notes: Returns None, True, or False each with 1/3 probability via randint(-1,1) dict lookup

misc.name: binary
misc.notes: Returns os.urandom(length) bytes if unseeded, else builds bytes list via randrange(256) loop

misc.name: md5
misc.notes: Hashes a random float string with hashlib.md5; returns hexdigest str or digest bytes depending on raw_output

misc.name: sha1
misc.notes: Hashes a random float string with hashlib.sha1; returns hexdigest str or digest bytes depending on raw_output

misc.name: sha256
misc.notes: Hashes a random float string with hashlib.sha256; returns hexdigest str or digest bytes depending on raw_output

misc.name: uuid4
misc.notes: Builds UUID4 from 128 random bits via uuid.UUID(int=..., version=4), optionally cast via callable

misc.name: uuid1
misc.notes: Assembles UUID1 from current timestamp plus random node/clock_seq/perturbation bits, optionally cast via callable

misc.name: uuid7
misc.notes: Assembles RFC-9562 UUID7 from current unix_ts_ms plus random rand_a/rand_b bits with version/variant fields, optionally cast via callable

misc.name: password
misc.notes: Builds password from configurable character categories, guarantees at least one char per enabled category by injecting required_tokens at random positions

misc.name: zip
misc.notes: Creates in-memory zip archive bytes with num_files random-binary files sized to fill uncompressed_size, with optional compression

misc.name: tar
misc.notes: Creates in-memory tar archive bytes with num_files random-binary files sized to fill uncompressed_size, with optional compression

misc.name: image
misc.notes: Generates a PIL image of given size/format filled with a random background color and a random polygon, returns bytes

misc.name: dsv
misc.notes: Generates delimiter-separated values string using csv.writer, expanding data_columns templates via pystr_format for each of num_rows rows

misc.name: csv
misc.notes: Delegates to dsv() with delimiter=',' to produce comma-separated values

misc.name: tsv
misc.notes: Delegates to dsv() with delimiter='\t' to produce tab-separated values

misc.name: psv
misc.notes: Delegates to dsv() with delimiter='|' to produce pipe-separated values

misc.name: json_bytes
misc.notes: Delegates to json() and encodes the result to bytes

misc.name: json
misc.notes: Recursively builds JSON structure from dict or list data_columns spec using _value_format_selection, serialises with json.dumps

misc.name: xml
misc.notes: Generates a pydict then wraps it under a random word key and serialises to XML via xmltodict.unparse

misc.name: fixed_width
misc.notes: Generates fixed-width text rows by expanding each data_columns definition via _value_format_selection and padding/truncating to specified column widths

passport.name: passport_dob
passport.notes: Delegates to self.generator.date_of_birth() and returns the result as a datetime.date (non-string return value, but is a public fake-value formatter).

passport.name: passport_owner
passport.notes: Conditionally parses first_name_male/first_name_female/first_name_nonbinary and last_name based on gender argument, returning a (given_name, surname) tuple.

passport.name: passport_number
passport.notes: Picks random element from passport_number_formats, substitutes ? with random uppercase letters via regex, then applies numerify for digit markers.

person.name: name_male
person.notes: Uses formats_male if present, else falls back to formats; parses chosen template via generator.parse

person.name: name_nonbinary
person.notes: Uses formats_nonbinary if present, else falls back to formats; parses chosen template via generator.parse

person.name: name_female
person.notes: Uses formats_female if present, else falls back to formats; parses chosen template via generator.parse

person.name: first_name_male
person.notes: Returns random_element(first_names_male) if attribute exists, else delegates to first_name()

person.name: first_name_nonbinary
person.notes: Returns random_element(first_names_nonbinary) if attribute exists, else delegates to first_name()

person.name: first_name_female
person.notes: Returns random_element(first_names_female) if attribute exists, else delegates to first_name()

person.name: last_name_male
person.notes: Returns random_element(last_names_male) if attribute exists, else delegates to last_name()

person.name: last_name_nonbinary
person.notes: Returns random_element(last_names_nonbinary) if attribute exists, else delegates to last_name()

person.name: last_name_female
person.notes: Returns random_element(last_names_female) if attribute exists, else delegates to last_name()

person.name: prefix
person.notes: Returns random_element(prefixes) if present; else combines prefixes_male+female+nonbinary or picks randomly between male/female lists; returns empty string if none exist

person.name: prefix_male
person.notes: Returns random_element(prefixes_male) if attribute exists, else delegates to prefix()

person.name: prefix_nonbinary
person.notes: Returns random_element(prefixes_nonbinary) if attribute exists, else delegates to prefix()

person.name: prefix_female
person.notes: Returns random_element(prefixes_female) if attribute exists, else delegates to prefix()

person.name: suffix
person.notes: Returns random_element(suffixes) if present; else combines suffixes_male+female+nonbinary or picks randomly between male/female lists; returns empty string if none exist

person.name: suffix_male
person.notes: Returns random_element(suffixes_male) if attribute exists, else delegates to suffix()

person.name: suffix_nonbinary
person.notes: Returns random_element(suffixes_nonbinary) if attribute exists, else delegates to suffix()

person.name: suffix_female
person.notes: Returns random_element(suffixes_female) if attribute exists, else delegates to suffix()

profile.name: simple_profile
profile.notes: Randomly selects sex if not provided, picks name_female or name_male accordingly, then assembles a dict with username, name, sex, address, free_email, and date_of_birth.

profile.name: profile
profile.notes: Builds a dict combining job, company, ssn, address, lat/lon tuple, random blood group from itertools.product, a list of 1-4 URLs, then merges simple_profile result and optionally filters to requested fields.

python.name: pyobject
python.notes: Dispatches to typed py* formatter based on object_type parameter, returns None if no type given

python.name: pybool
python.notes: Returns random_int(1,100) <= truth_probability, defaulting to 50% probability

python.name: pystr
python.notes: Joins random_letters of random length between min_chars and max_chars, prepends/appends optional prefix/suffix

python.name: pystr_format
python.notes: Parses a format string via generator.parse then applies bothify with optional letters set

python.name: pyfloat
python.notes: Constructs float from random left/right digit counts and sign, respecting min/max/positive constraints with overflow correction

python.name: pyint
python.notes: Returns generator.random_int(min_value, max_value, step) with defaults 0-9999

python.name: pydecimal
python.notes: Constructs Decimal from random sign, left integer part, and right digit string, clamped to min/max bounds

python.name: pytuple
python.notes: Wraps _pyiterable output as a tuple with configurable element count and value types

python.name: pyset
python.notes: Wraps _pyiterable output as a set with configurable element count and value types

python.name: pylist
python.notes: Wraps _pyiterable output as a list with configurable element count and value types

python.name: pyiterable
python.notes: Randomly returns one of pylist/pytuple/pyset with configurable element count and value types

python.name: pydict
python.notes: Zips unique random words as keys with _pyiterable values, capped at words_list_count

python.name: pystruct
python.notes: Builds a 3-tuple of (list, flat-dict, nested-dict) each populated with _random_type values keyed by random words

python.name: enum
python.notes: Returns random_element from the members list of the provided Enum class, raising on None/non-Enum/empty

sbn.name: sbn9
sbn.notes: Generates a 9-digit SBN by numerifying a random digit string, splitting registrant/publication via RULES lookup, computing a check digit via SBN9, and formatting with a separator.

user_agent.name: user_agent
user_agent.notes: Picks a random browser name from user_agents, then delegates to the matching method (chrome/firefox/safari/opera/internet_explorer) via getattr.

user_agent.name: chrome
user_agent.notes: Builds 5 platform-specific Chrome UA strings using randint for version/build/saf numbers plus platform token helpers, then returns one random element prefixed with Mozilla/5.0.

user_agent.name: firefox
user_agent.notes: Builds 5 platform-specific Firefox UA strings using randint, date_time_between, lexify/numerify for build tokens, and platform token helpers, then returns one random element prefixed with Mozilla/5.0.

user_agent.name: safari
user_agent.notes: Builds 3 platform-specific Safari UA strings using randint for saf/ver numbers, getrandbits for version format branching, locale, and platform token helpers, then returns one random element prefixed with Mozilla/5.0.

user_agent.name: opera
user_agent.notes: Picks Linux or Windows platform token via getrandbits, generates Presto/Version/Opera version numbers with randint, and combines them into a single Opera UA string.

user_agent.name: internet_explorer
user_agent.notes: Combines randint-generated MSIE version and Trident version with a random windows_platform_token into an MSIE-compatible Mozilla/5.0 UA string.

user_agent.name: linux_platform_token
user_agent.notes: Returns 'X11; Linux ' concatenated with a random element from linux_processors.

user_agent.name: mac_platform_token
user_agent.notes: Returns 'Macintosh; <mac_processor> Mac OS X 10_<randint(5,12)>_<randint(0,9)>' using a random mac_processors element.

user_agent.name: android_platform_token
user_agent.notes: Returns 'Android ' concatenated with a random element from android_versions.

user_agent.name: ios_platform_token
user_agent.notes: Picks a random apple_devices element and ios_versions element, then formats '<device>; CPU <device> OS <version_with_dots_replaced_by_underscores> like Mac OS X'.
